projectDescription: A global real-time weather application built with React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS that displays current conditions, hourly forecasts, and 7-day daily forecasts sourced from the Open-Meteo API.
keyFeatures: Global city search with debounced autocomplete via Open-Meteo Geocoding API
Real-time current weather: temperature, feels-like, humidity, wind speed, and cloud coverage
Hourly weather forecast for the next 7 hours from the current time
7-day daily forecast with high/low temperatures and weather icons
Dynamic day/night theme that switches the UI based on the selected location's solar time
Persistent city preference stored in localStorage across sessions
WMO weather code mapping to descriptive labels and Lucide React icons with day/night variants
Fully typed with TypeScript — all API responses and UI props are strictly typed
publicApiSurface: useWeatherContext() — React hook to consume the global WeatherContext
WeatherProvider — React context provider wrapping the entire app
useWeather({ latitude, longitude }) — hook fetching current/hourly/daily forecast
useGeocoding(city: string) — hook fetching city search results
useWeatherTheme(isDay: boolean | undefined) — hook toggling dark mode class
getWeatherIcon(code: number, isDay: boolean) — maps WMO code to Lucide icon + label
getCloudCoverage(cloudCover: number) — converts cloud % to readable string
formatDate(isoDate: string) — parses ISO date into fullDate / shortDate / hourOnly
isDayTime(currentDate, sunriseList, sunsetList) — determines day/night from solar data
weatherCodes — Record<number, WeatherData> mapping of all supported WMO codes
